What are the main credit card types? -
Best for: Frequent flyers and travel enthusiasts
• Enjoy luxury memberships with Club Marriott and EazyDiner.
• Complimentary Airport Lounge Access at select domestic lounges (2 per calendar quarter) and International lounge (1 per calendar quarter)
• Accelerated Rewards on travel-related spends — flights, hotels, and more.
• Annual Fee: First Year Free (conditions apply).
• Insurance Benefits: Complimentary travel accident and lost card liability cover.
• Xchange brands: 1:1 exchange on Apple imagine, Tanishq, Taj, M&S, The Body Shop.
Customers who travel frequently and want to accumulate travel points for flight tickets and enjoy airport lounge perks.
Focus: PowerMiles - Travel Rewards & Lounge Access
Reward Type: PowerMiles - Accelerated on Travel
How can I redeem Credit Card points for rewards? +
Equitas uses a rewards portal called Xchange (xchange.equitas.bank.in) for redeeming reward points.
You can redeem for products, vouchers, flight / hotel booking, etc. The card’s Terms & Conditions / MITC specifies the catalogue, valuation, and conditions for redemption.
The exact value per point / the minimum points required / where you can redeem depends on which card you have and which redemption option (product, voucher, travel) you select.
How do I apply for a Credit Card online? +
Visit the Equitas Bank Website’s Credit Cards section.
Choose the variant (TIGA, SELFE or PowerMiles) and click “Apply Now.” Or by giving missed call 1800 103 2977
You will be asked to provide KYC, identity, address, income proofs, etc., to complete an online / offline application form. (Standard card application flow implied)
The bank will verify your eligibility and documents and, if approved, dispatch the card. (You may also need to activate it with first transaction or PIN setup)
The application flow and decisioning may be instantaneous or take some time depending on internal checks.
What are the advantages of using Equitas Credit Cards? +
Here are the main benefits per card, plus general advantages:
• Premium metal card, luxury memberships (Club Marriott, EazyDiner) included
• Lounge access (domestic + international) depending on card rules
• 3× rewards on international spends, plus ability to convert points to airline / hotel miles
• Fuel surcharge waiver, detection and Xchange program redemption options.
• Flexibility of interest-free credit period (if you pay full within due date)
• Rewards / points for your everyday spending
• Perks like fuel surcharge waiver, insurance, lounge access (for premium)
• Ability to convert large purchases into EMIs (for TIGA)
• Multiple redemption options via Equitas’s rewards portal
Are there any charges for redemption of reward points? +
Yes, the Equitas “Most Important Terms & Conditions (MITC)” document lists several fees, and redemption governed by reward programme rules.
2.5% of the amount withdrawn (or ₹250, whichever is higher). Also, finance charges apply from day of withdrawal till full repayment.
• The bank may levy other charges (late payment, over limit, return of payment, etc.) as per the Schedule of Charges in the Card Agreement / MITC.

•  Redemption handling / administrative charges (if any) would be specified in the rewards programme terms (the MITC / catalogue) — it is possible that some redemptions may carry a small fee or conversion cost. (I didn’t find a publicly explicit “redemption fee” in the MITC I reviewed, so you will need to check the latest catalogue or rewards terms)
• TIGA: The annual fee may be waived if you spend a certain threshold in the anniversary year (as per terms)
• SELFE: Similarly, waiver if you cross a spending threshold (e.g., ₹2,00,000) in the year.
• PowerMiles: Waiver available if you spend a defined amount (e.g. ₹4,80,000) in the anniversary year.

So yes — while redemption may not always incur a direct separate fee (depending on the option), there are various fees built into the cards’ terms, and you must see the “Schedule of Charges / rewards programme” for your card variant.
How to register complaints related to my credit card? +
Register a complaint through the following ways:-
Write to customerservice@equitas.bank.in from your registered mail id
You can also lodge a complaint at RBI’s Sachet Portal
What memberships do I get with the PowerMiles Credit Card?
As a PowerMiles Credit Card holder, you are eligible to receive Club Marriott and EazyDiner memberships, which offer dining and lifestyle benefits through partner brands.

How are reward points calculated for my transactions?

Reward points, including accelerated rewards, are calculated based on the Merchant Category Code (MCC) assigned to the merchant where the transaction is made, which determines the applicable reward category.
What is a Merchant Category Code (MCC)?
An MCC is a four-digit code assigned to merchants based on their primary business activity, which helps the bank classify transactions and determine rewards eligibility.
Why is MCC important for earning rewards?
The MCC helps determine whether your transaction qualifies for accelerated rewards, as reward categories are mapped to specific MCC codes defined by the bank.
Which MCCs are included in the Dining category?
Transactions will be classified under Dining if the merchant falls under MCCs 5462, 5812, or 5814, as defined by the bank for reward eligibility.
Which MCCs are included in the Apparel category?
Transactions will be considered under Apparel if they fall under MCCs 5311, 5611, 5621, 5641, 5651, 5691, 5655, 5631, or 5699.
Which MCCs are included in the Grocery category?
Transactions will be categorized as Grocery if they fall under MCCs 5399, 5411, 5441, 5499, 5311, 5331, 5422, or 5451 as per the defined criteria.
Which MCCs are included in the Utility category?
Transactions will be considered under Utility if the merchant falls under MCCs 4814 or 4900.
Which MCC is applicable for Taxi category?
Taxi-related transactions will be classified under MCC 4121 for rewards eligibility.
Will all transactions in a category always earn accelerated rewards?
Not necessarily, as rewards are based on the MCC assigned to the merchant and not solely on the type of product or service purchased.
What if a transaction is classified under a different MCC?
If a transaction is tagged under a different MCC by the merchant’s acquiring bank, it may not qualify for accelerated rewards even if it appears to belong to a selected category.
Can the bank change the MCC of a transaction?
No, the MCC is assigned by the merchant’s acquiring bank or payment network and cannot be modified by the card-issuing bank.
What happens if a transaction does not match selected categories?
If a transaction does not fall under the selected categories based on MCC mapping, it will still earn base reward points but not accelerated rewards.

Minimum 500 points required for cashback, validity 24 months, ₹99 redemption fee applicable.
Can I change my billing cycle for my credit card?
Yes, you have the option to change your billing cycle for your credit card, allowing you to better align your billing and payment due dates with your financial planning needs.
How many times can I change my billing cycle?
You can request a billing cycle change only once per credit card, so it is important to carefully choose the most suitable option before confirming your request.
How can I request a billing cycle change?
You can request a billing cycle change through the Mobile Banking (MB) App or by contacting the Customer Care Centre (CCC), making the process simple and convenient.
What billing cycle dates are available for selection?
You can choose from the available billing cycle dates which include the 5th, 12th, 13th, 14th, 15th, 16th, 18th, 20th, and 25th of the month.
Can I select a billing cycle date outside the available options?
No, you can only select from the predefined billing cycle dates provided by the bank, and requests for any other dates will not be accepted.
When will the new billing cycle be applied?
The revised billing cycle will typically be implemented in the next billing period after your request is processed, depending on system timelines.
Can I change my billing cycle again later?
No, as per policy, only one billing cycle change is allowed per credit card, and further changes will not be permitted once it has been updated.
Will my payment due date change if I update my billing cycle?
Yes, your payment due date will change accordingly since it is directly linked to your selected billing cycle.
Is there any fee for changing the billing cycle?
Currently, there is no specific fee mentioned for changing the billing cycle, but customers are advised to check the latest terms and conditions or confirm during the request.
What factors should I consider before changing my billing cycle?
You should consider your salary credit date, repayment capacity, and existing financial commitments before selecting a new billing cycle, to ensure it aligns with your repayment convenience.
How are reward points calculated for my transactions?

Reward points, including accelerated rewards, are calculated based on the Merchant Category Code (MCC) assigned to the merchant where the transaction is made, which determines the applicable reward category.
What is a Merchant Category Code (MCC)?
An MCC is a four-digit code assigned to merchants based on their primary business activity, which helps the bank classify transactions and determine rewards eligibility.
Why is MCC important for earning rewards?
The MCC helps determine whether your transaction qualifies for accelerated rewards, as reward categories are mapped to specific MCC codes defined by the bank.
Which MCCs are included in the Dining category?
Transactions will be classified under Dining if the merchant falls under MCCs 5462, 5812, or 5814, as defined by the bank for reward eligibility.
Which MCCs are included in the Apparel category?
Transactions will be considered under Apparel if they fall under MCCs 5311, 5611, 5621, 5641, 5651, 5691, 5655, 5631, or 5699.
Which MCCs are included in the Grocery category?
Transactions will be categorized as Grocery if they fall under MCCs 5399, 5411, 5441, 5499, 5311, 5331, 5422, or 5451 as per the defined criteria.
Which MCCs are included in the Utility category?
Transactions will be considered under Utility if the merchant falls under MCCs 4814 or 4900.
Which MCC is applicable for Taxi category?
Taxi-related transactions will be classified under MCC 4121 for rewards eligibility.
Will all transactions in a category always earn accelerated rewards?
Not necessarily, as rewards are based on the MCC assigned to the merchant and not solely on the type of product or service purchased.
What if a transaction is classified under a different MCC?
If a transaction is tagged under a different MCC by the merchant’s acquiring bank, it may not qualify for accelerated rewards even if it appears to belong to a selected category.
Can the bank change the MCC of a transaction?
No, the MCC is assigned by the merchant’s acquiring bank or payment network and cannot be modified by the card-issuing bank.
What happens if a transaction does not match selected categories?
If a transaction does not fall under the selected categories based on MCC mapping, it will still earn base reward points but not accelerated rewards.
Is there a joining fee for the credit card?

Yes, a joining fee will be charged to the cardholder at the time of card issuance; however, this fee is eligible for reversal if the required spend condition is fulfilled within the specified timeline.
How can I get my joining fee reversed for Powermiles Card?
The joining fee will be reversed if you spend ₹1,20,000 within 90 days from the date of card activation, as per the defined eligibility criteria.
How will the joining fee be reversed?
The joining fee will be reversed in the form of cashback in the subsequent billing cycle after meeting the spend criteria, excluding applicable GST charges.
Will GST on the joining fee also be reversed?
No, GST applicable on the joining fee will not be reversed and will have to be borne by the cardholder as per government regulations.
What is the annual fee waiver condition for Powermiles Card?
The annual fee will be waived if you achieve a total spend of ₹4,80,000 in the preceding anniversary year of your credit card.
When will the annual fee waiver be applied?
The annual fee waiver will be evaluated based on your spending in the previous card anniversary year and will be applied accordingly in the subsequent cycle.
Will GST be reversed in case of annual fee waiver?
No, even if the annual fee is waived or reversed, the GST component will not be reversed and will continue to be payable by the cardholder.
What happens if I do not meet the spend criteria for fee reversal?
If the required spend criteria is not met within the given timeframe, the joining or annual fee will not be reversed and will be charged as applicable.
What is the annual fee waiver condition for Selfe credit card?
The annual fee for the credit card will be waived if the customer achieves a total spend of ₹2,40,000 in the preceding anniversary year of the card.
The preceding anniversary year refers to the 12-month period immediately before your card renewal date, during which your total spends are evaluated for waiver eligibility.
What happens if I do not reach ₹2,40,000 spend?
If your total eligible spends do not reach ₹2,40,000 in the preceding year, the annual fee will be charged as applicable and will not be waived.
Do all transactions count towards the annual fee waiver spend?
Only eligible transactions as defined by the bank will be considered for calculating the total spend required for the annual fee waiver.
What insurance benefit is available on my credit card?

Your credit card offers a Lost Card Liability cover of up to ₹10 lakhs(varies basis credit card ), protecting you against unauthorized transactions in case your card is lost or stolen, subject to policy terms.
Who is eligible for this insurance coverage?
This insurance benefit is applicable only to the primary cardholder and is not available for add-on or supplementary cardholders.
What is covered under the Lost Card Liability policy?
The policy covers unauthorized transactions made using your lost or stolen card, up to the specified coverage limit, as per the policy terms and conditions.
When does the insurance coverage become active?
The insurance coverage becomes active only after you complete either your first card transaction, PIN setup, or card activation.
Will I be covered if I have not activated or used my card?
No, the insurance benefit will not be active until the card is activated, PIN is set, or the first transaction is completed.
Is there a maximum coverage limit under this policy?
Yes, the Lost Card Liability coverage is available up to a maximum of ₹10,00,000 as per the policy terms.
Where can I check the detailed policy terms and conditions?
You can refer to the policy document provided by the bank at https://equitas.bank.in/complimentary-insurance-policy/ to understand detailed coverage, exclusions, and claim process.
What should I do if my card is lost or stolen?
In case your card is lost or stolen, you should immediately block your card through the mobile banking app or contact customer care and follow the claim process as per the policy.
How can I redeem my reward points?
You can redeem your reward points for travel bookings, gift vouchers, catalogue purchases, cashback against your card statement, or instant redemption at partner merchants.
What is the value of each reward point for a Selfe Card?
The value of each reward point varies by redemption type, with travel, vouchers, and catalogue redemptions valuing ₹0.35 per point, while cashback and instant redemption value ₹0.15 per point.
Is the reward point value the same for all redemption options?
No, the reward point value differs depending on the redemption option selected, with higher value typically available for travel and vouchers.
Is there a minimum number of points required for redemption?
Yes, a minimum of 500 reward points is required for redeeming points as cashback against your card statement.
Do reward points expire for Tiga and Selfe Card?
Yes, reward points expire after 24 months from the date they are earned if they are not redeemed within this period.
Do reward points expire for Powermiles Card?
Yes, reward points expire after 36 months from the date they are earned if they are not redeemed within this period.
Is there any fee for redeeming reward points?
Yes, a redemption fee of ₹99 per transaction will be charged whenever reward points are redeemed.
Can redeemed reward points be reversed?
No, once reward points are redeemed, they cannot be reversed, cancelled, or reissued.
Can I use reward points instantly while making a purchase?
Yes, you can use reward points for instant redemption at select partner merchants during transactions.
What happens if my reward points expire?
If reward points expire after 24 months for Tiga & Selfe and 36 months for Powermiles, they will be forfeited and cannot be reinstated or used later.
How do I earn reward points on my Powermiles credit card?
You will earn 3 reward points for every ₹100 spent on eligible retail transactions, helping you accumulate points on your everyday spending.
Do I earn additional rewards on international transactions on my Powermiles card?
Yes, you will earn 3X accelerated reward points on eligible international transactions, allowing you to earn rewards faster compared to domestic spends.
Are all transactions eligible for earning reward points?
No, transactions such as fuel, rent, wallet loads, government payments, cash advances, and EMI conversions are excluded from earning reward points unless specified otherwise.
What is the value of each reward point for a Powermiles Card?
The value of each reward point depends on redemption type: ₹0.50 per point for travel, vouchers, and catalogue, and ₹0.25 per point for cashback or instant redemption.

To enable card controls for your HDFC Bank Credit Card through NetBanking, follow these steps:
1. Log in to HDFC Bank NetBanking using your Customer ID.
2. Go to the Cards Tab >Select the card > Click Manage card>Manage Domestic and International Limits.
3. You will be able to view your existing daily domestic and international usage limits.
4. Enable or disable Online, Contactless, and International usage based on your preference.
5. Click ‘Update’, enter the OTP received on your registered mobile number, and then click ‘Continue’.
6. Your selected card controls will be updated instantly.
To enable card controls for your HDFC Bank Credit Card conveniently through our HDFC Bank MobileBanking App, follow these steps:
1. Open the HDFC Bank MobileBanking App and log in using your credentials.
2. Go to the ‘Cards’ section from the menu.
3. Select the HDFC Bank Credit Card for which you want to enable card controls.
4. Click ‘Manage Limits’, then enable/disable transaction types such as Online Transactions, Contactless Payments, and International Usage, or set transaction limits according to your preference.
5. Click ‘Update’ to confirm your changes and enter your MPIN. The updated card control settings will be applied instantly.
For more detailed management, you can also use our MyCards platform at https://mycards.hdfc.bank.in/ which offers a seamless experience without the need for passwords or downloads.
To enable card controls for your HDFC Bank Credit Card via MyCards, follow these steps:
1. Visit the MyCards portal- https://mycards.hdfc.bank.in/ on your desktop or mobile.
2. Log in using your registered mobile number and the OTP received.
3. For first-time users, enter the last four digits of your card.
4. On the card homepage, click the ‘Card Control’ tab.
5. Enable/disable features such as Online Transactions, Contactless Transactions, International Usage, and set usage limits as per your preference.
6. Confirm your settings, and the changes will be applied instantly.
You can conveniently enable card controls for your HDFC Bank Credit Card through PayZapp by following these steps:
1. Open the PayZapp app and log in using your credentials.
2. On the homepage, tap the ‘MyCards’ tab> Select ‘Go to MyCards’.
3. Log in using your registered mobile number and the OTP sent to your mobile.
4. If you are a first-time user, enter the last four digits of your HDFC Bank Credit Card.
5. On the card homepage, tap the ‘Card Control’ tab.
6. Enable or disable features such as Online Transactions, Contactless Transactions, and International Usage, or set usage limits according to your preference.
7. Confirm your settings. The updated card control settings will be applied instantly.
To enable card controls for your HDFC Bank Credit Card using WhatsApp Banking, follow the steps given below:
1. Add HDFC Bank’s official WhatsApp number – 7070022222 – to your contacts.
2. Send the text "Credit Card" to this number.
3. You will receive a reply with options. Choose the option “Manage My Card”
4. You will receive a reply with options to select “Domestic / International/ Enhance limits”
5. Choose the option you want to enable, such as Domestic Online Transactions, Contactless Transactions, or International Transactions.
6. Enter the transaction limit you want to set. An OTP will be sent to your registered mobile number. Enter the OTP to confirm the change.
7. Enter the last 4 digits of your Credit Card number.
8. The current status of the selected transaction type will be shown as Enabled.
To enable or modify your HDFC Bank Credit Card controls and transaction limits, you can contact HDFC Bank's toll-free numbers:
Toll-free numbers : 1800 1600 or 1800 2600 (available from 8 am to 8 pm)
By contacting our toll-free numbers, you can enable/modify limits for Domestic, International, Online, Contactless, ATM, and POS transactions on your Debit or Credit Card.
